description: Boomi helps our companies around the world to achieve concrete integration of each of the old platforms or tools that manage all our processes, this in order to unite and centralize all the information and processes and to find them all from the same place without having to be navigating everything and wasting valuable time. With Boomi, all our tools are intertwined due to the large amount of codes it supports.
author: Tony Ricardo R.
pros: The tool offers excellent BPM flows that allow us to improve the way we run our operations inside and outside the company, achieving a constant improvement in all products and services. The API management is extremely simple and intuitive, clearly you must have slight previous knowledge to carry out each of the changes. The tool does not consume so many resources for a highly technical software. The tool can be recommended to any entity or organization that needs to start integrating and centralizing its different tools and achieve a new evolutionary step in terms of being an organization. Boomi provides the necessary tools and knowledge to be able to carry out the different tasks and improve our companies, through one of the most important integration systems we have used.
cons: In some moments where we must generate scenarios that are slightly complex it is difficult to integrate the tool, which forces us to look for third party solutions to be able to carry it out without wasting time. Boomi's user interface is not as simple as expected. The performance graphs could offer a different way to see how things are happening. The price may be a bit high compared to other competitors.

description: I would say it has been overall very positive. When I have a problem I know I can reach SENSITIVE CONTENT HIDDEN and he gets back to me so quickly and resolves problems quickly as well. Resolutions are always solid recommendations, never a hack or a let me figure it out scenario.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Ease of use and company's commitment to the platform and its customers. Monthly updates fix and address problems we are facing and it feels really good to see all the successful customers at BOomi world.
cons: Flow is my primary con, only because we knew the Flow to Boomi Integrate Integration would be necessary but it seems like it may be difficult tech for the citizen Integrators.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Powerful Integration Platform with Great Visibility, but Costly at Scale
description: Overall, the combination of ease of use and strong data visibility makes Boomi a reliable and efficient integration platform, especially when managing multiple systems and ensuring data accuracy across them. The new dashboard with analytics brings in better insight on data transfers.
author: Rizwan K.
pros: Working with Boomi has been a great experience, particularly because of how easy it is to build integration processes. The low-code, drag-and-drop interface makes it very straightforward to design workflows, even for complex integrations. This significantly reduces development time and allows you to focus more on business logic rather than technical overhead. Another aspect I really value is the visibility it provides into data as it moves between systems. Boomi offers clear tracking of data packages, making it easy to monitor, troubleshoot, and audit integrations. You can quickly identify where an issue occurred, inspect payloads, and resolve problems efficiently, which is crucial in production environments.
cons: One of the main drawbacks of working with Boomi is the cost structure, particularly the high cost associated with adding new connections. As integrations scale across multiple systems, the pricing can increase significantly, which may be a concern for growing organizations or projects with tighter budgets. Additionally, the support experience could be improved. While documentation is available, resolving more complex or urgent issues through support can sometimes be slower than expected, which can impact timelines in critical situations.

review_title: Fast to build Can be difficult to fix
author: Simon L.
pros: Speed of development and range of connectors
cons: Frequent need to restart atoms. Crashes often.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: UI and customer support quick responses also the community
cons: very thin. not enough complex functionality natively resulting in complex groovy builds which leads to slow run time if volume is large

author: Eric L.
pros: the ability to fully customize the ETL process between our core business Systems
cons: The system is very well put together, but it is still a little heavy to use without the proper technical background

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: As someone who is new to integration, I was impressed by how easy it was to learn how to develop integrations on the Boomi Platform. The learning managment system and support/user guides were essential in jumpstarting my company's integration development.
cons: It is easy-to-use software. I would like to see improvements to Boomi Suggest, but I know it will be better over time due to the machine learning algorithms.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Getting Started and a great start
description: Overall it is a good start as we are learning to use it with our team internally
author: Benjamin D.
pros: I liked how we could quickly build integrations without needing to stand up a new environment each time
cons: The reporting leaves a lot to be desired. For me it takes so long to find issues when troubleshooting.

review_title: Easy to adopt and role out integration strategy platform
description: Very positive experience working with this product and the support from Boomi has been outstanding
author: Dee C.
pros: This platform was very easy to learn and roll out. How to stand up the infrastructure was understanding the requirements was well documented.
cons: The lack of ability to lock down file structures

review_title: Boosting Seamless Data Integration and Management with Boomi
description: I had positive experiences with Boomi, appreciating its comprehensive integration capabilities, ease of use, and ability to streamline data integration and management processes
author: Aditya R.
pros: There are several features and aspects of Boomi that users often appreciate like User-Friendly Interface, Broad Connectivity, Real-Time Monitoring and Alerts etc.
cons: Boomi is generally considered favourably, although there are some features, such as pricing structure and complexity for advanced configurations, that some users may find less appealing.

author: Xavier C.
pros: Integrations made simple! Boomi has streamlined our entire business and allowed us more time to focus.
cons: Nothing, it's an excellent tool and rightly rated A+.

description: If you lack of developers, is a good choice. You can train someone in no time so is able to create process for the company. For too complex process, it can be a challenge maintaining them. Is expensive...a lot, just recommended for big companies.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: There is no need of an advance developer to make simple but long process. Connect to different APIs protocols like HTTP, Odata, SFapi, etc. Connect to smtp or sftp/ftp servers and other external services. You can connect to almost anything and just moving the mouse.
cons: You cannot code, and sometimes that is bad... if you have performance issues you don't have to many options. You don't have code to refactor. The version control it's far from git or similar. I't very expensive, if you have developers, an app that make all that is way more cheaper that maintain a Boomi atom. It's very annoying not having a way to audit the connections, sometime the connectors fail and you don't have a good log to see what happen.

description: I started my career with Boomi technology and I am very glad to be apart of this wonderful technology so far. I love to part of Boomi ever.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I love Boomi's Gentle learning curve;their most user friendly UI;most interactive community;very useful user guide and process library and LMS. I like the new features and functionalities they incorporate in every releases. Boomi is the best Integration and Cloud tool I have ever used.
cons: Some things which needs their improvement like providing flexibility in running the same execution simultaneously with multiple extension values etc. But Very few cons. Their API Management should be improved.

review_title: The best solution to deploy changes automatically.
description: Various type of Integration activities like Monitoring and deployments.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Dell Bhoomi provides automatic support to deploy all the latest changes on a branch. It automatically checks for the latest changes checked in and then deploy automatically. It also provides automatic testing of various code modules. Monitoring and Event tracking also are the useful features.
cons: Dell Bhoomi is costly to use as various packages have different features. Advance features fall into the costly one.

review_title: Boomi is great for simple integrations, would like to see better organization/reporting
description: We have successfully tied together several financial platforms with Boomi as well as leveraged it for our master data management of customers. It has shown great promise in early applications and sparked ideas for many more integrations.
author: Jacob P.
pros: Boomi makes basic integrations extremely simple, non-technical staff are able to view and understand the integrations. There is a lot of power in the platform for complex integrations with the ability to expand using java. Ability to submit test cases is great and lowers the QA load for monthly releases.
cons: Process reporting has a lot of information but it isnt always easy to view. When managing errors on a mobile device we must request the desktop version to even view the error specifics. Reporting on overall usage of the platform can be a challenge currently, technology is a data driven world and I would like to have more robust reporting to view runs/errors/platform stats/ and deployed processes all in one place.

review_title: Very Good integration tool but need some improvements on specific area
description: I have very good experience of this tool on Application and Data Integration side. I love the way Boomi release and roll out the connectors and patch versions in all environments. However, I see some improvement needed on Application integration side related to Atom Queuing full compliant with JMS spec 2.0 version and also, some improvement needed on the Administration side for example there is no start/stop functionality on deployed process. Monitoring and management of Boomi process like if source or target system is down then automatically stop the bommi process and start it up automatically when target systems comes online.
author: Sujit N.
pros: Easy to Use In built Connectors
cons: 1) Date Function support is limited. 2) Lacks Dynamic process generation support - cannot read a database, obtain a result and then launch 1-N processes based on the results on a query. This forces us to fallback to DB Stored procedures for dynamic processing. 3) No field-level encryption option. 4) Improve dynamic handling of values on processes (e.g. Retry configuration) and Connection (e.g Timeout) 5) Have capability to integrate with Source Code Management tool 6) Ability to run a process with "Listener" starter in Test Mode 7) Email Alert generate if the deployed service or Listner is down. 8) JMS Message Selector usage in Atom Queue. 9) Ability to receive email alert by process

description: We are building integrations between applications to applications and extracting data from applications to end users much quicker than before.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: This platform enables us to develop and create integrations within a short period of time which is opposite of previous integration platforms or models with many lines of code.
cons: The platform is missing some component consistency across the platform. I think this could be easily solved and would rid of much frustration from a UX standpoint.

review_title: Go to Boomi if you want to innovate
description: Overall our experience with Boomi has been great. Integrations that have previously taken months to build, test and implement can now be done in weeks. Not only that but Boomi makes it easy to build modular integrations, so if one piece no longer works for you it can be easily replaced.
author: Trey Y.
pros: Boomi is a tool that can be picked up easily by just about anyone and allows for very quick development. Boomi makes prototyping integrations incredibly easy and quick and moving those solutions to production takes a few clicks.
cons: As with most graphical programming languages there are limitations as to what the tool can do, and while there may be workarounds that can be done in the system sometimes those carry performance implications.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: One of the best cross application integration platforms we have come across. Feature rich, user friendly, drag and drop functionality.
cons: Cost used to be low, but now is on the higher side We love everything else about this product

review_title: Used Boomi to connect our Newer versions of software to older Cloud legacy systems.
author: Geoff G.
pros: Was able to use the API connections with Boomi to integrate requests from acquired newer customer facing software to our existing legacy cloud systems to be able to handle the offerings without creating a new backend. This worked. It didn't always work great and it took some setting up, but once it was working it generally stayed working.
cons: Huge learning curve. You will need to leverage Dell's partner offerings and train up Boomi experts within your organization through Dell Boomi classes. Reading through and interrupting the Logs is speciality all its own aside from the knowledge needed to setup the interfaces in the first place. If it breaks, you will likely need help in fixing it.

description: Dell Boomi makes Integration with Healthcare hospitals and clinics very easy for us. We can do a typical integration in hours or days rather than weeks or months as with traditional integration engines.
author: Brian S.
pros: Easily able to integrate out of the box. Powerful enough to use for complex use cases.
cons: There is nothing we do not like about Dell Boomi.

description: Overall it is very easy tool to learn and develop integration with other applications. Checkpoint for debugging is very impressive for trouble shooting the problem.
author: kalaiselvan K.
pros: Dell Boomi is an component based drag and drop tool for different types of integration. We can do the development in browser based cloud dell boomi UI with different set of components and adapters. UI is very simple to develop and deployment is very friendly with inbuilt version control . We can use Groovy and Javascript for custom logic business handling. Components functionality avoid developers to spend much time on coding
cons: The number connectors/adapters available for integration with heterogeneous application is limited. Since development happens over browser in cloud, any dis-connectivity with internet will block the development.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Dell Boomi allows you to do low code development to create integration between multiple applications. It is a simple drag and drop interface that can be picked up by any developer. There is also an extensive knowledge base and an active community that helps support this platform. So far we are are still in the process of deploying our first project but I can definitely see where we can utilize this going forward for multiple software.
cons: Dell Boomi has limited integration with our ERP System QAD and we appear to be the first company to take a look at EDI with Dell Boomi and QAD. While this is exciting, I'm sure that we will have challenges as we navigate these new waters.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Having focused on middleware and process automation for quite some time, I have had the opportunity to review and use a number of platforms over the years. These range from incredibly cumbersome to overly simplistic and virtually unusable; on-prem to cloud and hybrid models. For years integration/middleware vendors have advertised that their product can be configured by the business analyst although this has been a reach at best. Boomi realizes this goal while also providing the ability to generate complex time sensitive integrations that leverage user defined code where needed. Boomi provides a comprehensive and growing support community to complement their internal product support that has been nothing but stellar. Response is quick and robust. While there is always more to be learned, Dell's Boomi is the product that we all envisioned but until now has been unavailable.
author: Scott W.
pros: Ease of use.
cons: Nothing yet.

review_title: Boomi has been crucial for scaling real time and Batch based data transfer Services
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Boomi suggest and on premise molecule providing a highly available application
cons: Active /passive disaster recovery capability
